notes: Spec asserts the CORRECT behavior (not weakened to the bug): a gemini-cli foreground subagent (forced via @generalist) writes its own transcript while the parent is alive; the daemon should observe it (transcript_new), link it (parent_linked), and on a SIGKILL of the parent reap both parent and child without a spurious working transition for the orphaned child (issue #321). KNOWN_FAILING today (daemon=bug), live-confirmed 2026-06-12: the child transcript materializes at ~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/<parentId>/<childId>.jsonl (a BARE-UUID basename in a NESTED per-parent subdir, NOT chats/session-<childId>.jsonl). The daemon's FilesUnderRoot watches chats/*.jsonl at the top level only, so it never emits transcript_new for the nested child (child_session_birth fails) and has no deriveParentSession rule for this layout (parent_linked fails) — the child is not observed at all, not merely unlinked. The parent's own lifecycle (ready->working) and the SIGKILL reaping (process_exited) ARE observed and pass; the no-spurious-working invariant for the (unobserved) child holds vacuously. parent_killed/child_cleanup are anchored on parent_turn_start (a landed phase) so the two known_failing phases do not cascade. The 1-child shape is sufficient: the no-working-transition invariant is identical for any N>=1.

text: Parent gemini session appears in ready
The @generalist prompt drives the parent ready -> working
KNOWN_FAILING: the subagent transcript materializes at chats/<parentId>/<childId>.jsonl but the daemon watches chats/*.jsonl top-level only, so it emits no transcript_new for the nested child
KNOWN_FAILING: detector should bind child.session_id -> parent.session_id, but there is no gemini parent-link rule and the child is unobserved -> no parent_linked
SIGKILL on the parent: pid_manager emits process_exited for the parent session

invariants: no state_transition to working for child after parent_turn_start
no state_transition to waiting for child after parent_turn_start
text: The orphaned child is reaped without a spurious working/waiting transition (issue #321 invariant; holds vacuously since the child is unobserved)
